vector.question: What is the German tank problem?
What is the final estimator for Buffon's needle?
What are the learning outcomes from the coupon collector problem?
After solving the lighthouse problem, what probability distribution describes the observed position on the shore?
What challenge in AI does the secretary problem demonstrate?
What special case makes the weighted mean converge to the ordinary mean?
What is the MLE for a Poisson distribution with different sized intervals?
What special case makes the MLE for a Poisson distribution with different sized intervals converge to the sample mean?
What is the final unbiased estimator for the German tank problem?
What is the crossing condition in Buffon's needle?
What are the MLEs for the mean and variance of a normal distribution?
Why does weighting observations by inverse variance make sense?
How do we estimate the total number of tanks from only the largest observed serial number?
Why is the maximum likelihood estimate in the German tank problem biased?
Why does the coupon collector problem involve harmonic numbers?
What is the idea behind rejecting the first group of applicants in the secretary problem?
How does the lighthouse problem transform a random angle into a position on the shore?
Why does Buffon's needle let us estimate pi using random throws?
Why do different interval sizes change the Poisson maximum likelihood estimate?
Which estimator is based on the largest observed serial number, and why is it biased if used directly?
How can the gap between observed serial numbers be used to estimate the total population size?
Why does adding the average missing gap to the maximum observed value reduce bias?
What is the intuition behind giving smaller variance measurements more influence in an average?
Why do inverse variances appear as weights when combining multiple noisy measurements?
What happens to the combined estimate when one observation has much lower uncertainty than the others?
Which problem estimates pi using random geometric intersections?
How can repeated random needle drops be turned into an estimate of a mathematical constant?
Why does the probability of crossing a line depend on both the needle angle and its distance from the nearest line?
Which derivation uses a change of variables to convert position uncertainty into a probability density?
Why does the lighthouse problem produce a heavy-tailed distribution rather than a normal-looking one?
How does Bayes' rule allow the unknown source position to be estimated from observed flashes?
What is the expected waiting time until all possible outcomes have been seen at least once?
Why does collecting the final unseen item take much longer on average than collecting the first few?
How can the total waiting time be decomposed into a sum of geometric waiting times?
Which strategy involves rejecting an initial sample and then accepting the first later option that beats everything seen so far?
Why does the optimal stopping threshold end up being close to a fixed fraction of the total number of candidates?
What is the trade-off between learning from early candidates and leaving enough later candidates to choose from?
How does the likelihood change when Poisson observations come from intervals of different sizes?
Why is the rate estimate adjusted by the total exposure time rather than just the number of observations?
Why is the sample mean the maximum likelihood estimate for the mean of a normal distribution?
What role does minimizing squared error play in estimating the mean of a Gaussian?
Why does the maximum likelihood variance estimate divide by n rather than n minus one?
